en.extract:
The Tucumán mountain finch (Poospiza baeri) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to shrubby woodland in the Andes of north-western Argentina, but it is possible its distribution extends marginally into adjacent Bolivia. Together with the closely related Cochabamba mountain finch, it is placed in the genus Poospiza. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''Tucumán mountain finch''' ('''''Poospiza baeri''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Thraupidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to shrubby woodland in the [[Andes]] of north-western [[Argentina]], but it is possible its distribution extends marginally into adjacent [[Bolivia]]. Together with the closely related [[Cochabamba mountain finch]], it is placed in the genus ''[[Poospiza]]''.<ref>Nores, M. 2008. ''[http://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCprop366.html Resurgir el Género Compsospiza.] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130411010641/http://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCprop366.html |date=2013-04-11 }}'' South American Classification Committee. Accessed 27-04-2009.</ref> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
